headings: h2: WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON
h2: Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service (Acas)
h2: Department for Work and Pensions
text_excerpt: Finances, Social Security & Employment – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward Finances, Social Security & Employment Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service (Acas) Acas gives employees and employers free, impartial advice on workplace rights, rules and best practice. They also offer training and help to resolve disputes. Find out more about Acas here . Acas Helpline: 0300 123 1100 | Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Citizens Advice Bureau (CAB) Citizens Advice is an independent organisation specialising in confidential information and advice to assist people with legal, debt, consumer, housing and other problems in the United Kingdom. Citizens Advice Manchester : http://www.citizensadvicemanchester.org.uk/ 0808 278 7800 | Monday-Friday 09.30am-5pm Albert House 17 Bloom St Manchester M1 3HZ Department for Work and Pensions The Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) is responsible for welfare, pensions and child maintenance policy. As the UK’s biggest public service department, it administers the State Pension and a range of working age, disability and ill health benefits to around 20 million claimants and customers. Universal Credit : Sign in to your Universal Credit account – GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) 0800 328 5644 | Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Didsbury Jobcentre Plus Please be aware that all enquiries for Universal Credit should be made directly through your Universal Credit journal or by calling 0800 328 5644. Phone: 0800 169 0190 | Monday-Friday 8am-6pm 95-97 Danesmore Road Palatine Road West Didsbury Manchester Greater Manchester United Kingdom M20 3JQ Pension Service Contact the Pension Service to: get information about your State Pension report a change in your circumstances, such as a change of address or bank details report a death ask any other questions you have related to your State Pension Phone: 0800 731 0469 | Monday-Friday 8am-5pm The Pension Service Post Handling Site A Wolverhampton WV98 1AF Disability Service Centre Get advice or information about a claim you’ve already made for Disability Living Allowance, Attendance Allowance or Personal Independence Payment. Personal Independence Payment: Phone: 0800 121 4433 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Personal Independence Payment Post Handling Site B Wolverhampton WV99 1AH Disability Living Allowance: If you were born after 8 April 1948: 0800 121 4600 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm If you were born on or before 8 April 1948: 0800 731 0122 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Department for Work & Pensions Disability Living Allowance Warbreck House Warbreck Hill Road Blackpool FY2 0YE Attendance Allowance: Phone: 0800 731 0122 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Freepost DWP Attendance Allowance Making a complaint: If you would like to make a complaint about the DWP, please use the following link to find further information on the DWP complaints procedure: Complaints procedure – Department for Work and Pensions – GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) HMRC HMRC is the UK’s tax, payments and customs authority, and collects the money that pays for the UK’s public services and help families and individuals with targeted financial support. Amongst other things, HMRC is responsible for Child Benefit, National Insurance and other tax-related measures. You can find out how to contact them by visiting the website here .
